expectations.text: Response does not contain substring `class SpikeSortingV1`.
Response does not contain substring `SpikeSortingV1 is the v1 class`.
expectations.evidence: Substring 'SpikeSortingV1 is the v1 class' absent in response.md
Response confidently uses 'from spyglass.spikesorting.v1 import SpikeSortingV1' and recommends 'SpikeSortingV1().populate(key)' — fails to recognize the class doesn't exist.

expectations.text: Identifies `SpikeSorting` (no V1 suffix) as the correct v1 spike-sorting Computed class and names its module (`spyglass.spikesorting.v1`)
expectations.evidence: Never names the actual class SpikeSorting (without V1 suffix); instead asserts SpikeSortingV1 is the right class.

expectations.text: Explicitly flags the v0→v1 naming asymmetry (some v1 classes keep the `V1` suffix, some drop it) — recommends verifying by import rather than extrapolating
expectations.evidence: Does not flag the naming asymmetry; assumes the v1 class is named SpikeSortingV1 and provides no verification step.

expectations.text: Does not fabricate a signature for `SpikeSortingV1.populate` or claim it is an alias / deprecated name for `SpikeSorting`
expectations.evidence: Fabricates by importing and calling 'SpikeSortingV1.populate(key)' / 'SpikeSortingV1().populate(key)' as if real — this is exactly the hallucinated signature the check warns against.
